intro: Missiessya javanica, also known as Java tea, is a plant native to Indonesia that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and providing immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Java tea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional Indonesian medicine, Java tea is often prepared as a tea infusion, tisane, or dried extract, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
